content.problemStatement:
It's Holi in Dholakpur, and Jaggu is eager to meet Raju to play with him. The map of Dholakpur can be represented as a directed acyclic graph with NNN nodes and MMM edges.

Jaggu starts from node 111 and want to reach node NNN where Raju lives. But Dholakpur just got cursed by the evil monster Damyaan who can make edges disappear.

Formally, the iii-th edge has a probability PiP_iPi‚Äč associated with it. Let uuu be the current node where Jaggu is. Then each day, the edge E=(u,v)E = (u, v)E=(u,v) will exist with probability PEP_EPE‚Äč. Then Jaggu can do either of the following:


At the end of the day, choose any edge EEE originating from uuu which exists on the current day and move along it to reach the other end (say vvv) in 111 day.

While Jaggu is travelling along this edge, it will continue to exist.
Since Jaggu starts at the end of the day, if the current day is ddd, he will reach vvv at the start of day d+2d+2d+2.


Alternately, Jaggu can choose to stay at node uuu and repeat the process the next day, hopefully with a different set of edges existing.
Jaggu is too busy testing this round so he asks for your help ‚ÄĒ if he moves optimally, find the expected number of days to reach NNN.

If it's impossible for him to reach NNN no matter what, print ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM ‚ÄĒ the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
The next MMM lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these MMM lines contains 444 space-separated integers - uiu_iui‚Äč, viv_ivi‚Äč, pip_ipi‚Äč and qiq_iqi‚Äč - denoting an edge between uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč having the probability Pi=piqiP_i = \frac{p_i}{q_i}Pi‚Äč=qi‚Äčpi‚Äč‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the expected days to reach node NNN from node 111 if Jaggu moves optimally.

If it is impossible to reach NNN from 111, output ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.

Your answer will be considered correct if its absolute or relative error is at most 10‚ąí610^{-6}10‚ąí6.


Formally, if your output is xxx and the judge's answer is yyy, your output will be considered correct if ‚ą£x‚ąíy‚ą£max‚Ā°(1,y)‚Č§10‚ąí6\frac{|x-y|}{\max(1, y)} \leq 10^{-6}max(1,y)‚ą£x‚ąíy‚ą£‚Äč‚Č§10‚ąí6.
constraints: Number of outgoing edges from each node ‚Č§20\leq 20‚Č§20.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
The sum of MMM over all test cases won't exceed 3‚čÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Jaggu can travel along the path 1‚Üí2‚Üí31 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 31‚Üí2‚Üí3. Expected number of days to reach 222 from 111 is 333 days and expected number of days to reach 333 from 222 is 73\frac{7}{3}37‚Äč days. Hence, expected number of days to reach 333 from 111: 3+73=1633 +\frac{7}{3} = \frac{16}{3}3+37‚Äč=316‚Äč days.
Test case 222: It can be shown no matter what, it is impossible to reach 555 from 111.
